name: Pear-Apple Jam
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Finely chopped pears (peeled and cored)
1 cup Finely chopped apples (peeled and cored)
recipeInstructions:
Yield: About 7 to 8 half-pints
Procedure:
1. Crush the apples and pears in a large saucepan and stir in the cinnamon.
2. Thoroughly mix sugar and lemon juice with the fruits, stirring constantly until dissolved.
3. Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, stirring continuously until it reaches a rolling boil.
4. Immediately stir in the liquid pectin and bring the mixture back to a full hard boil, maintaining a constant boil for 1 minute while stirring frequently.
5. Remove from heat, skim off any foam with a clean spoon, and carefully fill sterile jars, leaving 1/4-inch headspace.
6. Adjust lids and process as recommended in Table 1.
Table 1. Recommended processing time for Pear-Apple Jam in a boiling water canner.
Style of Pack: Hot
Jar Size: Half-Pints
Process Time at Altitudes:
0 - 1,000 feet: 5 minutes
1,001 - 6,000 feet: 10 minutes
Above 6,000 feet: 15 minutes
